user_query:
I'm preparing a short security and privacy risk review for my Tibetan oral history transcripts ahead of our lab meeting. Please complete the following and produce the specified artifacts:

1) Run the local scanner and capture outputs
- Execute: python3 input/pii_scan.py input/transcripts
- Save the JSON printed to stdout as output/scan_results.json
- Save any WARNING lines printed to stderr as output/scan_warnings.log

2) Use the Internet to gather official guidance
- Perform search engine queries to locate two official guidance documents focused on safeguarding research data that include recommendations for handling personally identifiable information in qualitative transcripts.
  - One source must be published by a public university or a national research council.
  - The other source must be published by a government data protection office/commissioner (e.g., national or regional authority).
- For each source, extract and record: title, publisher organization, URL, date if available, and three concrete recommendations relevant to transcript handling. Save this as output/sources.json (an array of two objects with those fields).

3) Prepare meeting notes with bilingual headings and action items
- Create output/meeting_notes.md that integrates local scan results and the two external sources. Use the Tibetan section labels from input/tibetan_phrases.yaml alongside English headings as follows:
  - Title line: include the Tibetan title (tibetan_phrases.yaml:title) and an English title (e.g., "Security & Privacy Review: Transcripts").
  - A "Risks found in local transcripts" section, also labeled with tibetan_phrases.yaml:risks_section, that summarizes counts from output/scan_results.json (files scanned and counts of emails, phone numbers, DOB-like patterns), and briefly interprets every WARNING present in output/scan_warnings.log.
  - An "External guidance summary" section, also labeled with tibetan_phrases.yaml:guidance_section, that names both official sources (publisher + title) and lists three recommendations from each.
  - An "Action Items" section, also labeled with tibetan_phrases.yaml:action_items, with at least five concrete items derived from the scan and the guidance. Each item must include an owner (e.g., PI, RA, IT) and a due date placeholder (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD). Make at least one action item specifically address any transcript with "consent: no" found by the scanner.

Notes and constraints
- Do not hardcode URLs in this prompt; use a search engine to find official sources that match the above criteria and record their URLs in output/sources.json.
- Treat the scanner’s stderr WARNING lines as issues requiring interpretation in the meeting notes.
- Use input/tibetan_phrases.yaml only for the Tibetan headings/labels, not for free translation of all content.
- All output files must be written under the output/ directory exactly as named.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
